Title: A specialized flavone biosynthetic pathway has evolved in the medicinal plant, Scutellaria baicalensis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 4'-deoxy root-specific flavones (chrysin, baicalein, baicalin, wogonin, wogonoside)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive medicinal flavones with reported antitumor, antioxidant, and antiviral properties
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not identified as a specialized determinant; endogenous/general CHI activity is sufficient for pinocembrin formation in pathway reconstruction
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Only one CHI transcript was identified in roots, suggesting no novel CHI isoform dedicated to root-specific flavone biosynthesis. 2. Transient pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana showed SbCHI was not required for pinocembrin or chrysin production when SbCLL-7 and SbCHS-2 were present. 3. The authors infer that pinocembrin chalcone can be converted by general CHI activity or spontaneously.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern: A root transcript was identified, but no specialized root-specific CHI isoform was found
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Origin_Species: A root transcript was identified, but no specialized root-specific CHI isoform was found
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq identification; transient pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves, with or without cinnamic acid supplementation
Common_Genes.Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter in transient expression constructs
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA300475; SRP068883; SRS1263237; SRX1546377; SRR3123399)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as optional supporting gene in pathway reconstruction, but not essential because host CHI activity may suffice
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Excluded from the specialized core determinant set by the authors because no dedicated CHI isoform for RSF biosynthesis was found.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 CYP93B25; flavone synthase II
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbFNSII-2 is highly root-expressed and strongly correlates with root-specific flavone accumulation. 2. RNAi silencing of SbFNSII-2 drastically reduced baicalin, wogonoside, baicalein, and wogonin and caused accumulation of pinocembrin-derived intermediates. 3. Recombinant enzyme assays showed SbFNSII-2 specifically converts pinocembrin to chrysin and does not accept naringenin or eriodictyol. 4. Heterologous expression in Arabidopsis produced chrysin only when pinocembrin was supplied. 5. Pathway reconstruction in tobacco confirmed SbFNSII-2 is a core enzyme in the specialized 4'-deoxyflavone pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 4'-deoxy root-specific flavones (especially chrysin-derived baicalein, baicalin, wogonin, wogonoside)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA300475; SRP068883; SRS1263237; SRX1546377; SRR3123399)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step; terminal determinant for entry into the specialized 4'-deoxyflavone branch from pinocembrin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes NADPH-dependent dehydrogenation/desaturation of pinocembrin to form the flavone chrysin
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter in Arabidopsis and Nicotiana benthamiana heterologous expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious penalty reported; lower catalytic activity than SbFNSII-1 despite strict specialization
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream; committed branch step converting 4'-deoxyflavanone intermediate to 4'-deoxyflavone scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Origin_Species: Highly expressed in roots; much lower in stems, leaves, and flowers; induced 4.8-fold by methyl jasmonate in hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assigned CYP93B25; evolved recently after duplication from SbFNSII-1 and neofunctionalized for pinocembrin specificity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive medicinal flavones with reported antitumor, antioxidant, and antiviral properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes synthesis of chrysin and downstream 4'-deoxy root-specific flavones; silencing sharply decreases baicalin, baicalein, wogonoside, and wogonin
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Highly expressed in roots; low in aerial organs; induced by methyl jasmonate
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate treatment; pinocembrin feeding in Arabidopsis; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with or without cinnamic acid
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering gene for production of chrysin and downstream medicinal 4'-deoxyflavones when combined with upstream pinocembrin-supplying genes
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the immediate flavone scaffold precursor required for downstream baicalein/wogonin glycoside biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbCLL-7 is preferentially expressed in roots in a pattern matching root-specific flavone accumulation. 2. Recombinant protein assays showed SbCLL-7 is highly specific for cinnamic acid and has much greater catalytic efficiency for this substrate than other tested Scutellaria CLLs. 3. RNAi silencing of SbCLL-7 reduced baicalin, wogonoside, and baicalein in hairy roots. 4. Transient expression with SbCHS-2 in tobacco generated pinocembrin, confirming its role in supplying cinnamoyl-CoA to the specialized pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA300475; SRP068883; SRS1263237; SRX1546377; SRR3123399)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step; upstream entry step bypassing C4H to direct flux into the 4'-deoxyflavone pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates cinnamic acid by ligating CoA in an ATP-dependent reaction to produce cinnamoyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter in Arabidopsis and Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty reported; overexpression alone in Arabidopsis did not produce pinocembrin because compatible CHS activity was absent
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Origin_Species: Highest in roots; at least threefold lower in aerial tissues; not significantly induced by methyl jasmonate
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains a C-terminal SKL peroxisomal targeting motif according to discussion; recruited from a CoA ligase-like ancestor distinct from Arabidopsis CNL/BZO
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive medicinal flavones with reported antitumor, antioxidant, and antiviral properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies cinnamoyl-CoA for pinocembrin formation and is required for full accumulation of root-specific 4'-deoxyflavones
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Predominantly in roots; lower in stems, leaves, and flowers
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate treatment; transient pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana with or without cinnamic acid supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical upstream engineering target to divert phenylpropanoid flux from cinnamic acid into medicinal 4'-deoxyflavones, especially when stacked with SbCHS-2 and SbFNSII-2
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for the specialized end-product branch

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Chalcone synthase; type III polyketide synthase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbCHS-2 is strongly expressed in roots and is the root-associated CHS isoform linked to root-specific flavone biosynthesis. 2. In transient Nicotiana benthamiana assays, coexpression of SbCHS-2 with SbCLL-7 produced pinocembrin, showing SbCHS-2 can use cinnamoyl-CoA in vivo. 3. Addition of SbFNSII-2 converted this flux into chrysin, establishing SbCHS-2 as an essential core enzyme of the specialized pathway. 4. Expression is strongly induced by methyl jasmonate.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA300475; SRP068883; SRS1263237; SRX1546377; SRR3123399)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch enzyme in the specialized pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses cinnamoyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA units to form pinocembrin chalcone, which is converted to pinocembrin
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter in Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious penalty reported; activity appears specialized relative to flower-expressed SbCHS-1
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream, immediately downstream of cinnamoyl-CoA formation and upstream of chrysin formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Origin_Species: Highly expressed in roots and weakly in flowers; induced about 16-fold by methyl jasmonate
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A root-enriched CHS isoform, highly similar to previously reported Scutellaria CHS2 alleles; diverged from SbCHS-1 relatively recently
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive medicinal flavones with reported antitumor, antioxidant, and antiviral properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables pinocembrin production from cinnamoyl-CoA and is required for reconstruction of chrysin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High in roots, low in flowers; induced by methyl jasmonate
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate treatment; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with or without cinnamic acid supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential engineering component for establishing the 4'-deoxyflavone pathway in heterologous hosts alongside SbCLL-7 and SbFNSII-2
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the immediate precursor pool (pinocembrin) for the target flavone branch
